gateway.badge: Immersive Exhibition – Akira · Ghost in the Shell
gateway.question: Are you truly human?

hero.subtitle: Beyond the human
hero.quote: "When flesh disappears, what remains of humanity?"
hero.akiraContent: Created in 1982 by Katsuhiro ÅŒtomo, Akira emerges from a thriving Japan, still marked by nuclear trauma. Here, technology is no longer progress, but a mutating force that dehumanizes the body. Through brutal metamorphosis where flesh and machine merge, the work questions us: by forcing our own evolution, don't we risk turning humanity into mere remnant?
hero.gisTitle: Ghost in The Shell (1989 - 1991): Spirit Beyond Flesh
hero.gisContent: Created in 1989 by Masamune Shirow, Ghost in the Shell explores a future where the boundary between human and machine completely disappears. In this hyper-connected society, the body is merely a cybernetic shell that can be enhanced or replaced. Here, progress is no longer an endured mutation, but an aspiration to leave the flesh. By freeing ourselves from biological limits, the work confronts us with the ultimate dilemma: what remains of our humanity when our spirit dissolves into infinite data?

experiences.title: Experiences
experiences.description: Embark on a thrilling race to collect all the cyborg fragments, and avoid enemy vehicles if you want to reach your destination...
experiences.reservationSub: Tickets available from March 15 to June 30, 2026
experiences.promoText: A reward awaits you at the finish line.

transition.title: Beyond the human
transition.subtitle: An exhibition about the limits of the body and consciousness

manga.title: Manga Archive
manga.description: Explore the universes of Akira and Ghost in the Shell through the pages of these manga classics.
manga.mainTitle: Beyond the human: an immersive exhibition
manga.hook: This immersive exhibition plunges you into the heart of this duality: between Akira's mutating flesh and Ghost in the Shell's digital consciousness.
manga.pitch: Through an immersive journey, we invite you to cross the boundary of reality to explore the limits of the human. Between monumental projections and soundscapes, immerse yourself in the universe of these two iconic mangas.

podcast.title: Podcasts
podcast.description: Listen to our expert analysis on the central themes of the exhibition.

infoPratique.title: Practical information
infoPratique.description: Discover all the practical details to prepare your visit to the museum. Opening hours, pricing, access, accessibility information and answers to your frequently asked questions.
infoPratique.pricingContent:
Full price - 9 euros
Reduced rate - 6 euros
Child and person with disability - Free
infoPratique.hoursContent:
Tuesday - Saturday: 9am - 9pm
Sunday: 9am - 7pm
Monday: Closed

Closed on January 1st, December 25th, and public holidays
infoPratique.accessContent:
Metro line 8.
Paid parking nearby.
Bike access: Velib' station 200m away.
infoPratique.accessibilityContent:
Museum admission is free for persons with disabilities and one companion upon presentation of proof.

Priority and direct access to the museum's reception and security controls for persons with disabilities and their companion (or family members).
infoPratique.faq1Question: Do I need to book in advance?
infoPratique.faq1Answer: Yes, it is strongly recommended to book your tickets online to guarantee access to your desired time slot.
infoPratique.faq2Answer: Yes: students, job seekers and persons with disabilities receive a discounted rate upon presentation of proof.
infoPratique.faq3Answer: Yes, you just need to log in and access your profile to cancel your reservation.
